differentialBuckets.label: Adrenal Insufficiency: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup

source.source_section: Adrenal Insufficiency generated endocrine workup: diagnostic frame
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

differentialBuckets.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL.

differentialBuckets.action: Use local assay and patient context; do not apply numeric anchors without clinical interpretation.

redFlags.label: Shock, vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyponatremia/hyperkalemia, altered mental status.

redFlags.action: Escalate urgently, reassess severity, and evaluate for dangerous mimics or complications.

requiredQuestions.label: Fatigue, weight loss, anorexia, nausea/vomiting, abdominal pain, salt craving.

source.source_section: Adrenal Insufficiency generated endocrine workup: diagnostic workup
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

initialTests.action: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L.

initialTests.label: Cosyntropin stimulation test.
